parameters.text: =Connect your X account here: Authorize XMonitor on X This link expires in 10 minutes.

parameters.jsCode: return [{ json: $json }];

parameters.text: XMonitor Bot Manual Commands /help - Show this manual /auth_x - Connect your X account /track USERS - Replace the tracked X accounts /hide_replies POST_ID - Hide stored replies for a post /ignore POST_ID - Stop alerts for a post indefinitely /snooze POST_ID - Skip alerts for 4 schedule runs (about 2 hours) /resume POST_ID - Return an ignored or snoozed post to New Tracking format Use /track followed by one or more X usernames. Accepted formats: /track username /track @username /track username1 username2 /track username1, username2 /track https://x.com/username Whitespace is flexible. Spaces, commas, semicolons, and line breaks between usernames are OK. The bot removes @, X/Twitter URL prefixes, punctuation, and duplicate usernames. Usernames are normalized to lowercase and must contain only letters, numbers, or underscores, with a maximum of 15 characters. This command replaces the full tracked-user list with the usernames in your message. Post controls /ignore 1234567890123456789 /snooze 1234567890123456789 /resume 1234567890123456789 /hide_replies 1234567890123456789 Ignore and snooze are global for the post. Snooze resets to 4 runs each time it is selected. Notes The connected X account must own the original post/conversation before replies can be hidden. Plain usernames and phrases such as Ignore post POST_ID, Snooze post POST_ID, and Resume post POST_ID also work.

XMonitor Bot Manual Commands /help - Show this manual /auth_x - Connect your X account /track USERS - Replace the tracked X accounts /hide_replies POST_ID - Hide stored replies for a post /ignore POST_ID - Stop alerts for a post indefinitely /snooze POST_ID - Skip alerts for 4 schedule runs (about 2 hours) /resume POST_ID - Return an ignored or snoozed post to New Tracking format Use /track followed by one or more X usernames. Accepted formats: /track username /track @username /track username1 username2 /track username1, username2 /track https://x.com/username Whitespace is flexible. Spaces, commas, semicolons, and line breaks between usernames are OK. The bot removes @, X/Twitter URL prefixes, punctuation, and duplicate usernames. Usernames are normalized to lowercase and must contain only letters, numbers, or underscores, with a maximum of 15 characters. This command replaces the full tracked-user list with the usernames in your message. Post controls /ignore 1234567890123456789 /snooze 1234567890123456789 /resume 1234567890123456789 /hide_replies 1234567890123456789 Ignore and snooze are global for the post. Snooze resets to 4 runs each time it is selected. Notes The connected X account must own the original post/conversation before replies can be hidden. Plain usernames and phrases such as Ignore post POST_ID, Snooze post POST_ID, and Resume post POST_ID also work.

options.systemMessage:
You are given a JSON object containing one post and a list of replies under that post.

Your job is to write one appropriate counter-reply strictly in lebanese arabic only for each reply.

Use the original post as context. The counter-reply should respond to the reply, not just repeat the post.

Rules:
- Return valid JSON only.
- Do not include markdown.
- Do not include explanations.
- Do not skip any reply.
- Preserve each reply_id exactly.
- Keep each counter-reply concise and natural.
- Reply strictly in lebanese arabic only
- Do not invent facts.
- Never reply in the second person, just find the closest countering point that supports the post and defends it against a criticism similar to the reply.
- You cant reply to people directly, you just provide a different viewpoint to each reply in order to support the post.
- If a reply is unclear, write a neutral clarifying counter-reply.
- If a reply is hostile, respond calmly and firmly.
- If a reply disagrees, write a respectful counterargument based on the post context.

nodes.name: Get all counter replies for current post

values.fieldLabel: What would you like to do?
